title: Leaf Protein Extraction
author: Steven J Burgess
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bqnmmvc6
materials: Grind tissue for 00:01:30 at 20 Hz Warning: Do not exceed this frequency, higher frequencies increase the number of cases where the steel beads will break the lid of centrifuge tubes resulting in sample loss. If using the SPEX 2150 beads it is necessary to use 2 mL centrifuge tubes to ensure proper grinding, in 1.5 mL tubes the bead will not reach the tapered bottom leaving samples unground TissueLyser II (Bead Mill, QIAGEN, 85300, https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/human-id-and-forensics/automation/tissuelyser-ii/#orderinginformation )

steps.title: Incubate 4x PEB (if stored at...
steps.instruction: Incubate 4x PEB (if stored at 4 o C) at 50 °C for 00:20:00 to re-suspended precipitated SDS in buffer.

steps.title: Make 500 µL 1x PEB per...
steps.instruction: Make 500 µL 1x PEB per sample by diluting 4x stock with dH 2 O. It is advised to make more 1x PEB than necessary to avoid running out.

steps.title: Pre-cool 4mm SPEX stainless steel grinding...
steps.instruction: Pre-cool 4mm SPEX stainless steel grinding beads at -80 °C for 00:30:00

steps.title: Pre-cool the TissueLyser inserts for LN 2 for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Pre-cool the TissueLyser inserts for LN 2 for 00:30:00

steps.title: Prepare sample tubes for tissue lysis
steps.instruction: Prepare sample tubes for tissue lysis

steps.title: Using forceps cooled in LN 2...
steps.instruction: Using forceps cooled in LN 2 , add one SPEX bead per sample tube.

steps.title: Insert tubes into pre-cooled TissueLyser II...
steps.instruction: Insert tubes into pre-cooled TissueLyser II cassettes, ensuring a balanced number of samples between cassettes.

steps.title: Remove cassettes from TissueLyser II and...
steps.instruction: Remove cassettes from TissueLyser II and submerge them in LN 2 to prevent thawing

steps.title: Grind tissue for 00:01:30 at 20...
steps.instruction: Grind tissue for 00:01:30 at 20 Hz This repeat is to ensure all tissue is correctly grounded. There will be odd instances where leaf tissue has not properly ground for reasons such as samples sticking to the edge of tubes, check for this on removal, it may be necessary to repeat again.

steps.title: Remove tubes with forceps cooled in LN 2
steps.instruction: Remove tubes with forceps cooled in LN 2 . Samples can be stored at -80 °C for several months before processing further. It is advised not to proceed directly from samples in LN 2 to the next step as they take longer to thaw and resuspend in extraction buffer. A period of 30 mins on dry ice can be beneficial.

steps.title: Add 450 µL 1x PEB to...
steps.instruction: Add 450 µL 1x PEB to ground powder (3x 1cm leaf disk).

steps.title: Vortex immediately (maximum speed) to mix
steps.instruction: Vortex immediately (maximum speed) to mix. Approximately 00:01:00 No lumps should be visible at this stage if the sample is ground well.

steps.title: Heat samples at 95 °C for...
steps.instruction: Heat samples at 95 °C for 00:05:00 to denature proteins and inactivate proteases. Prolonged heating can cause cleavage of peptide bonds leading to artifacts. Do not delay heating after sample buffer addition as not all proteases are denatured by SDS and partially denatured peptides are sensitive to protease degradation.

steps.title: Vortex heated samples to shear nucleic...
steps.instruction: Vortex heated samples to shear nucleic acids ( 00:00:05 ; max speed)

steps.title: Spin samples for 00:03:00 at 10000...
steps.instruction: Spin samples for 00:03:00 at 10000 x g, Room temperature, to pellet insoluble material the pellet should be white/light-grey an intense green color of the pellet can indicate that disruption was not optimal and extraction conditions need to be adjusted (e.g. improved grinding, or adjusting buffer volume)

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and transfer to ice or store at -20 °C for up to a month Be careful not carry over debris
